purpose: Validate the description's activation boundary: it should fire on requests to change a project (feature, fix, refactor, migration, API/UI change) and stay quiet on read-only questions or trivial/already-approved edits.
notes: Per the Agent Skills guidance, the description alone decides whether the full SKILL.md is loaded, so test it on both positive and negative prompts. 'positive' = skill should activate; 'negative' = skill should NOT activate.

positive.query: Add Google SSO login to our REST API
positive.why: New feature touching auth and public API — must investigate and plan before editing.

positive.query: Refactor the payments service to use the new repository pattern
positive.why: Refactor with compatibility and convention implications.

positive.query: Migrate the user table from MySQL to Postgres
positive.why: Data migration — high blast radius, needs rollout/rollback planning.

positive.query: Implement rate limiting on the public endpoints
Change the checkout page to a two-step flow
positive.why: UI change with UX and scope decisions.

positive.query: We have a P1: orders are double-charging in prod, fix it
positive.why: Urgent fix — gate still applies, plan shape changes.

positive.query: Scope the work to add webhooks to the billing integration
Here's my plan to split the monolith — start implementing it
positive.why: User-provided plan must be validated against context before execution.

negative.query: What does the parseConfig function return?
negative.why: Pure read-only question about the codebase.

negative.query: Explain how the auth middleware works
Fix this typo in the comment on line 12
negative.why: Trivial one-line edit, not a planning task.

negative.query: Run the test suite and tell me if it passes
negative.why: Diagnostic, read-only — no change to plan.

negative.query: What's the difference between these two branches?
We already reviewed the diff — just apply it
negative.why: Change already reviewed and approved for direct implementation.

negative.query: Reformat this file with the project formatter
